reviews.review_description: For those familiar with apps like: Tilt Brush, Dream Brush  or Meta Brush, THIS is the updated and perfected version of those apps! Not only did this developer add lots of extra features but they also added multiplayer for up to 6 users in as their Meta Avatars! Our school is going to buy multiple copies for our students and have their VR Art teacher pop in-between their private rooms to help them with their projects. We are also  going to have the students work on VR art projects together in the same Multi-Brush rooms! I cant recommend this app enough! A+++

reviews.review_title: No refund, think twice before purchase
reviews.review_description: App is quite good. But no import, export functions. No more updates or further services.
Takes literally 10m to start on Q3... Barely works. Nothing new. You can get a better game for FREE!

As an artist and VR enthusiast, I was excited to try out Multibrush, a virtual reality painting app.

From the moment I opened the app, I was impressed by the intuitive interface and the wide range of brushes and pens available.

With everything from traditional options like pens and pencils to more experimental options like neon lights and particle effects, there's something for every artistic style.

What really stood out to me about Multibrush was the thoughtful evolution of the app.

The developers have clearly put a lot of effort into improving the user experience and addressing issues.

For example, I had a problem with saving my work, but the team quickly resolved it with an update.

In addition to being a great painting app, Multibrush also offers the added bonus of multiplayer functionality.

My friends and I had a blast creating art together, and it was awesome to see random people show up and join in on the fun.

However, there are a few areas where the app could be improved. 

For example, it would be nice to have a way to stream music or access to an MP3 player while creating. 

It would also be helpful to have avatars and the ability to customize headset colors to differentiate between users.

Overall, I would highly recommend Multibrush to anyone interested in virtual reality painting. 

It's a fantastic app with a lot of potential for growth. And don't forget to use the 25% off coupon link when purchasing it! 

https://www.oculus .com/appreferrals/xinwix/3438333449611263/?utm_source=2

—

The Oculus Quest 2 has a wide range of single player games, as well as many co-op multiplayer games that can be enjoyed with family, friends and loved ones, even if you're not in the same location. 

VR technology brings people together in a unique and exciting way.

If you want to purchase additional headsets for family members, loved ones, or friends, you can use the €30 credit available through the coupon code link (https://www.oculus .com/referrals/link/xinwix/) to get the credit after activating the headset.

This credit can be used to purchase at least 1-3 games or co-op games to get them started.

This is a great opportunity to share the virtual reality experience with others.

Some very thoughtful and useful updates to the user experience from Tilt Brush. I can appreciate the passion and amount of energy the developers have made to get the project to this stage. Like a few, I initially had an issue getting the Multiplayer function working but swapping the Wi-Fi to a Mobile hotspot on one device fixed it. Looking forward to the continued evolution of this excellent application.
Yesss!!!!!!

What else could you want in life? 

On a more serious note, this is great for remotely collaborating on projects with a group, especially for things that need precise modeling or just a quick 3d drawing. It's also very nice to just hop in with friends and mess around drawing random stuff. 

Tilt brush has always bothered me, because while it's open source, Google decided that they have to take advantage of the fact that there is no other way to get tilt brush on the Quest, so they can charge for it. This is Tilt brush, built with the actual Tilt Brush source code, WITH other features like Multiplayer, and more to come if I read the description properly. 

Thanks to Oculus for not being stupid and letting this on their store.
Seems to work great, but the servers are pretty dead

My friends and I go in and draw, but we need a way to stream music while we create. Can you build in access to SoundCloud or an MP3 player that plays music for the whole room! And... where are our avatars? We’d love to see these kind of updates to make the experience even better!

This would easily be a 5 star review if a left-handed option was added. I really enjoy using this, but it can be somewhat awkward drawing with my non-dominant hand.

Edit: I have just found out about the left-handed mode. This is awesome, though I would suggest that it be made easier to find/explained somewhere in the app. I've changed my review to 5 stars.
fun to explore

reviews.review_title: New avatars are awful in every way
reviews.review_description: I have played this game for almost a year and have been fine with a majority of the updates and changes, but the recent addition of the avatar system is awful in every way, not only do they look creepy and uncanny but they also have terrible performance and will make the game stutter and lag extremely badly. The avatars simply do not fit into the game, they feel so off putting and I think there should be an option to go back to using the floating headset instead of these disturbing avatars.

developer_response.body: Hi. Avatars are optional, you can disable them on a per-room basis. There is a toggle that turns avatars on or off for the room in the room creation panel. We will add an option to turn them off globally (even in rooms that have them enabled) in a future update.

reviews.review_description:
Had this game for 7 months and i’m super upset this price has been changed from free to $20. The magic of multibrush was meeting people in vr, and telling them to download multibrush and come play with me, and they always did. This game is essentially pointless to me now. Not to mention they’re charging $20 for Tiltbrush with multiplayer, since this game is sourced from Tiltbrush by Google, and the only change is the multiplayer aspect. 


TLDR: a fun free game that I would enthusiastically tell my friends to download, and hang out with them in, has been ruined by a $20 paywall.

developer_response.body: Hi, we definitely understand where you're coming from. We created MultiBrush out of love for Tilt Brush and a desire to see continuing improvement and growth. Setting a price was necessary in order to fund further development, and we will be rolling out new features like Oculus Avatars, hand tracking, and passthrough support over the next few months. If you want to share the experience with someone for whom the price is a barrier, please just send us an email at multibrush@rendever.com and we would be happy to help out!

reviews.review_description: After installed you have to restart your quest2 and redraw the guardian then the multi-player works fine. :)

This is drawing in 3d. I feel that the openbrush is better and run smoother than multi brush at that time I am writing this. While it is free, I have 4 stars for not running smoothly and make me dizzy

developer_response.body: Hi, there have been performance improvements recently, please let us know if you still find MultiBrush to be slow. We can be reached at multibrush@rendever.com, thanks!

reviews.review_description: This application is AWESOME!

This is awesome. The controls and colors on this are brilliant! I like the single player aspect as well as the Multiplayer. Please keep them both.

DEVELOPERS: I didn't see a left-handed mode on this. Could we get one? Also, I hope you will be able import original artwork into this for both Multi and single player. That would make this a five-star game.

developer_response.body: Hi! You can switch handedness by tapping the bottom of both controllers together. We're looking at the import of photos and 3d models as a feature, hopefully we can get it into a future update!

reviews.review_description: 5 stars

developer_response.body: Glad you like it! Not sure if this was intentional, but you left us a one-star rating with a 5-star comment! :)

reviews.review_title: Get this game if you like drawing
reviews.review_description: It is amazing I love this
